speaker: CHORUS
text: Now entertain conjecture of a time

speaker: CHORUS
text: When creeping murmur and the poring dark

speaker: CHORUS
text: Fills the wide vessel of the universe.

speaker: CHORUS
text: From camp to camp, through the foul womb of night,

speaker: CHORUS
text: The hum of either army stilly sounds,

speaker: CHORUS
text: That the fixed sentinels almost receive

speaker: CHORUS
text: The secret whispers of each other’s watch.

speaker: CHORUS
text: Fire answers fire, and through their paly flames

speaker: CHORUS
text: Each battle sees the other’s umbered face;

speaker: CHORUS
text: Steed threatens steed in high and boastful neighs

speaker: CHORUS
text: Piercing the night’s dull ear; and from the tents

speaker: CHORUS
text: The armorers, accomplishing the knights,

speaker: CHORUS
text: With busy hammers closing rivets up,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Give dreadful note of preparation.

speaker: CHORUS
text: The country cocks do crow, the clocks do toll,

speaker: CHORUS
text: And, the third hour of drowsy morning named,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Proud of their numbers and secure in soul,

speaker: CHORUS
text: The confident and overlusty French

speaker: CHORUS
text: Do the low-rated English play at dice

speaker: CHORUS
text: And chide the cripple, tardy-gaited night,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Who like a foul and ugly witch doth limp

speaker: CHORUS
text: So tediously away. The poor condemnèd English,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Like sacrifices, by their watchful fires

speaker: CHORUS
text: Sit patiently and inly ruminate

speaker: CHORUS
text: The morning’s danger; and their gesture sad,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Investing lank-lean cheeks and war-worn coats,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Presenteth them unto the gazing moon

speaker: CHORUS
text: So many horrid ghosts. O now, who will behold

speaker: CHORUS
text: The royal captain of this ruined band

speaker: CHORUS
text: Walking from watch to watch, from tent to tent,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Let him cry, “Praise and glory on his head!”

speaker: CHORUS
text: For forth he goes and visits all his host,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Bids them good morrow with a modest smile,

speaker: CHORUS
text: And calls them brothers, friends, and countrymen.

speaker: CHORUS
text: Upon his royal face there is no note

speaker: CHORUS
text: How dread an army hath enrounded him,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Nor doth he dedicate one jot of color

speaker: CHORUS
text: Unto the weary and all-watchèd night,

speaker: CHORUS
text: But freshly looks and overbears attaint

speaker: CHORUS
text: With cheerful semblance and sweet majesty,

speaker: CHORUS
text: That every wretch, pining and pale before,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Beholding him, plucks comfort from his looks.

speaker: CHORUS
text: A largesse universal, like the sun,

speaker: CHORUS
text: His liberal eye doth give to everyone,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Thawing cold fear, that mean and gentle all

speaker: CHORUS
text: Behold, as may unworthiness define,

speaker: CHORUS
text: A little touch of Harry in the night.

speaker: CHORUS
text: And so our scene must to the battle fly,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Where, O for pity, we shall much disgrace,

speaker: CHORUS
text: With four or five most vile and ragged foils

speaker: CHORUS
text: Right ill-disposed in brawl ridiculous,

speaker: CHORUS
text: The name of Agincourt. Yet sit and see,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Minding true things by what their mock’ries be.
